3.why: We build node objects before serializing.

4.why: The walk fetches nodes by id.
4.invariant: p_by[i] is p's node at slot i.

7.why: Each real value becomes a node.

9.happening: Create p node {i} with empty children.
9.invariant: A fresh node has no children.

10.happening: Is a slot left for a left child?

12.happening: Link slot {pc} as the left child.

14.happening: Is a slot left for a right child?

16.happening: Link slot {pc} as the right child.

20.why: Same build for the second tree.

21.why: The walk fetches q's nodes by id.
21.invariant: q_by[i] is q's node at slot i.

24.why: Each real value becomes a node.

26.happening: Create q node {i} with empty children.
26.invariant: A fresh node has no children.

27.happening: Is a slot left for a left child?

29.happening: Link slot {qc} as the left child.

31.happening: Is a slot left for a right child?

33.happening: Link slot {qc} as the right child.

37.why: We flatten p to a preorder list.
37.invariant: sp collects p's preorder with nulls.

39.happening: Seed the stack with p's root ({pn} nodes).
39.invariant: st starts with the root or None.

40.invariant: sp is p's preorder so far.

41.invariant: nd is the position to emit.

42.happening: Is this position empty (a missing child)?
42.invariant: Empty positions still emit a slot.

43.happening: Emit a null marker (∅) into sp.
43.why: It records the gap so structure is captured.

45.happening: Emit this node's value into sp.

46.why: It comes after the left in preorder.
46.invariant: Right is emitted after the left subtree.

47.why: It comes first in preorder (popped next).
47.invariant: Left is emitted before the right.

48.invariant: sq collects q's preorder with nulls.

49.invariant: st2 holds q positions to emit.

50.happening: Seed it with q's root ({qn} nodes).
50.invariant: st2 starts with q's root or None.

51.invariant: sq is q's preorder so far.

52.invariant: nd is the position to emit.

53.invariant: Empty positions still emit a slot.

54.happening: Emit a null marker (∅) into sq.

56.happening: Emit this node's value into sq.

59.happening: Assume the two serializations are equal.
59.why: We only flip this on a difference.

60.happening: Do the two serializations differ in length?
60.invariant: Equal lengths are required to match.

61.happening: Lengths differ — the trees are not the same.
61.why: One tree has more positions than the other.

63.happening: Sweep both lists while they still agree.
63.why: Any differing cell ends the match.

64.happening: Do the cells at position {i} differ?
64.why: Equal serializations match at every position.

65.happening: The cells differ — the trees are not the same.
65.why: A mismatch at position {i} was found.

67.happening: Return whether the two serializations match: {same}.

init.happening: Building both trees and starting p's serialization.
init.why: We need node objects before flattening.

loop.happening: Flattening a tree to preorder, or sweeping the two lists.
loop.why: Serialize then compare element by element.
loop.invariant: Each pass processes the whole structure.

check.happening: Testing an empty position, a length difference, or a differing cell.

update.happening: Emitting a value or ∅, or recording the verdict.
update.why: The serializations grow; same tracks equality.
update.invariant: sp and sq capture structure + values.

move.happening: Popping a position or advancing the compare cursor.
move.why: The walks and the sweep move forward.

return.happening: Returning whether the serializations are equal.
